markdown:
BERT uses the Transformer as an **encoder**, specifically a multi-layer bidirectional Transformer encoder.

According to the original BERT paper, "BERT's model architecture is a multi-layer bidirectional Transformer encoder based on the original implementation described in Vaswani et al. (2017)." [1] This is reinforced by the paper's own description that "BERT stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers." [1]

A key distinction from decoder-based models like OpenAI GPT is also made explicit: "the BERT Transformer uses bidirectional self-attention, while the GPT Transformer uses constrained self-attention where every token can only attend to context to its left." [1] Figure 3 in the paper reiterates this: "BERT uses a bidirectional Transformer. OpenAI GPT uses a left-to-right Transformer." [2]

The paper also notes that BERT's encoder-only design has practical implications: "not all tasks can be easily represented by a Transformer encoder architecture, and therefore require a task-specific model architecture to be added." [3]

Sources
[1] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 2.2 Unsupervised Fine-tuning Approaches, p. 3
[2] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, A.1 Illustration of the Pre-training Tasks, p. 13
[3] BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding, 5.3 Feature-based Approach with BERT, p. 9
